title: Im going to show Neon Genesis Evangelion
display_title: Im going to show Neon Genesis Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: I'm going to show Neon Genesis Evangelion             Quote Post by chiefen » Thu Oct 06, 2005 10:09 am I'm going to show Evangelion to a friend of mine. Thing is, when we reach episode 21, should I show him the Director's Cut versions of 21-24 or the regular ones? Showing both is out of the question.

[]: Re: Okay, I'm gonna show NGE (don't say EVA >_< ) to a             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Oct 06, 2005 10:49 am chiefen wrote: mine. Thing is, when we reach episode 21, should I show him the Director's Cut versions of 21-24 or the regular ones? Showing both is out of the question.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Oct 06, 2005 12:58 pm I agree with Shin-seiki; if nothing else, seeing the real-world coffee-pot scene will help reduce the confusion whern Shinji strangles Asuka for the first time.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Oct 06, 2005 1:04 pm For once im with shin on somthing show him the DC's

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Oct 06, 2005 6:20 pm But should he show him the TV endings before End Of Evangelion or not?

[]:             Quote Post by insight » Thu Oct 06, 2005 6:25 pm I should probably not judge the mental capabilities of this unknown person. But I would recommend showing him EoE before the tv ending, so he doesn't shy away from watching the alternate ending after the series. My friend watched the tv ending and turned it off half-way through 26 because he got bored with it. I guess it depends on the person. I loved both endings, EoE was better IMO because it showed what was happening in much much more detail than the series.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Oct 06, 2005 6:38 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: But should he show him the TV endings before End Of Evangelion or not?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Oct 06, 2005 8:57 pm Watch the real thing. 1-26, then maybe the DCs, then maybe Death, then The End of Evangelion. I don't like this idea of putting the DCs over the original, that's like saying that you shouldn't watch 25-26 in favor of EoE. Watch the entirety of the series in its original format.

[]: ^_^ \/,,             Quote Post by chiefen » Thu Oct 06, 2005 9:26 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: But should he show him the TV endings before End Of Evangelion or not?

[]: Re: ^_^ \/,,             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Oct 06, 2005 10:23 pm chiefen wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: Watch the real thing. 1-26, then maybe the DCs, then maybe Death, then The End of Evangelion.

[]: Re: ^_^ \/,,             Quote Post by Digitalex » Fri Oct 07, 2005 10:46 am thewayneiac wrote: It's not the same thing at all! Anno wasn't happy with the quality of these episodes, so he fleshed them out with additional material, but basically they are the same episodes. EOE is all new material, and though it tells the same story with the same conclusion, it is not the same production. Plainly you need to see both renditions of the ending. I'll never understand the fondness you and Carl Horn have for the broadcast versions of these episodes. Well like it or not, the Directors Cuts are the official version; the broadcast episodes are only on Renewal/Platinum for completeists.

[]:             Quote Post by Shnooks » Fri Oct 07, 2005 2:38 pm I showed my friend EoE before ep. 25-26. Not only did she have a severe breakdown, but she didn't really understand what was going on. I take the series as more of a...desensitization method before the movie. Showing someone EoE before seeing the series is like walking out in the snow naked. You have no idea what just happened.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Fri Oct 07, 2005 3:41 pm True Shooks. It was tricky enough figuring it out with all the background info supplied within the series. It would be a total mindfuck-o-rama if you watched that flick without some knowledge of the series beforehand.

[]: Re: ^_^ \/,,             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Oct 07, 2005 11:18 pm thewayneiac wrote: It's not the same thing at all! Anno wasn't happy with the quality of these episodes, so he fleshed them out with additional material, but basically they are the same episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Oct 08, 2005 1:17 pm Your purism will be your undoing.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Oct 08, 2005 1:51 pm How do you figure? By the way, what happened to the title of this thread?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Oct 08, 2005 4:19 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: How do you figure?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sat Oct 08, 2005 4:20 pm Nothing.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Oct 08, 2005 4:22 pm If you're going to watch Neon Genesis Evangelion, you should watch Neon Genesis Evangelion . And if someone is too ficle to only watch one version of it, then he's obviously not going to care enough to comprehend it anyway. So you might as well show him the real deal. And if he does get hooked, there will be more for him to explore.

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Tue Oct 11, 2005 9:28 am thewayneiac - It's not the same thing at all! Anno wasn't happy with the quality of these episodes, so he fleshed them out with additional material, but basically they are the same episodes. EOE is all new material, and though it tells the same story with the same conclusion, it is not the same production. Plainly you need to see both renditions of the ending.
